nodeMixNote: Blackwell/Rubin on TSMC N3/N3P with CoWoS packaging; Rubin production expected H2 FY2027
rationale: NVDA 10-K (FY2026, filed 2026-02-25): Data Center revenue surged to drive total revenue of ~$130B (implied by scale of Compute & Networking segment); NVDA states it places non-cancellable inventory orders "in advance of historical lead times" and pays premiums/deposits to secure supply, with manufacturing supply commitments of the largest commitment category on the balance sheet — implying effectively sold-out allocation; AMD 10-Q (Q1 2026, filed 2026-05-06): Data Center revenue grew 57.2% YoY ($5,775M vs $3,674M), with total unconditional purchase commitments of $25.7B as of March 28, 2026 signaling aggressive forward capacity locking, and ATMP JV purchases up 44% YoY ($718M vs $497M), all consistent with sustained allocation-constrained demand across the AI accelerator silicon bottleneck stage.
